title: Display artist profile name in 'Uploaded by' label

requirements.summary: Replace raw principal identifiers with artist display names in 'Uploaded by' labels across the app
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Uploaded by' section on ContentCard shows the uploader's profile display name, not a raw principal or generic label.
If the uploader has no display name set, fall back gracefully (e.g., show a shortened principal or 'Unknown Artist').
The same fix applies consistently wherever 'Uploaded by' is rendered across the app (e.g., ContentDetailPage if applicable).
The display name links or navigates to the uploader's user profile page.

file_operations.description: Add a new React Query hook `useGetUserProfile(user: Principal)` that wraps the backend's `getUserProfile` method to fetch a specific user's profile by their principal. This will be used to look up uploader profiles in the ContentCard and ContentDetailPage components.

Update the ContentCard component to fetch and display the uploader's profile display name using the new `useGetUserProfile` hook with the `content.uploader` principal. Replace the current 'Uploaded by' section to show the artist's name instead of a raw principal. If the profile is loading, show a skeleton or placeholder. If the profile has no name or fetch fails, fall back to a shortened principal (first 8 characters) or 'Unknown Artist'. Make the artist name clickable and navigate to `/profile/{uploader-principal}` using the Link component from TanStack Router.

Update the ContentDetailPage component to fetch and display the uploader's profile display name using the new `useGetUserProfile` hook with the `content.uploader` principal. Add or update any 'Uploaded by' or artist information display to show the artist's name instead of a raw principal. If the profile is loading, show a skeleton or placeholder. If the profile has no name or fetch fails, fall back to a shortened principal (first 8 characters) or 'Unknown Artist'. Make the artist name clickable and navigate to `/profile/{uploader-principal}` using the Link component from TanStack Router.
